channel.title: AP Physics C Knowledge Graph
channel.description: Last 10 notes on AP Physics C Knowledge Graph

item.title: Example 33 - Rod on Rails with EMF Source
item.description: Example 33: Conducting Rod on Rails Driven by EMF Source Problem Statement Suppose a conducting rod (mass m, resistance R) rests on two frictionless and resistanceless parallel rails a distance \ell apart in a uniform magnetic field \vec{B} (perpendicular to the rails and rod) as in Figure 29–53.

item.title: Example 32 - Conducting Rod on Rails Motor Braking
item.description: Example 32: Conducting Rod on Rails — Magnetic Braking Problem Statement A conducting rod rests on two long frictionless parallel rails in a magnetic field \vec{B} (perpendicular to the rails and rod) as in Figure 29–53.

item.title: Example 31 - Square Loop Near Wire Flux and EMF
item.description: Example 31: Square Loop Near a Wire — Flux, EMF, and Force Problem Statement (a) Determine the magnetic flux through a square loop of side a if one side is parallel to, and a distance b from, a straight wire that carries a current I.

item.title: Example 30 - Power Line Induction Coil
item.description: Example 30: Power Line Induction — Farmer’s Coil Problem Statement A power line carrying a sinusoidally varying current with frequency f = 60 Hz and peak value I_0 = 125 A runs at a height of 7.0 m across a farmer’s land.

item.title: Example 29 - Loop Pulled from Magnetic Field
item.description: Example 29: Force to Pull a Loop from a Magnetic Field Problem Statement Part of a single rectangular loop of wire with dimensions shown is situated inside a region of uniform magnetic field of 0.650 T.

item.title: Example 34 - Circular Loop in Diverging Field
item.description: Example 34: Force on a Current Loop in a Diverging Magnetic Field Problem Statement A circular loop of wire, of radius r, carries current I.

item.title: Example 28 - Current-Carrying Wires Suspended by Strings
item.description: Example 28: Current-Carrying Wires Suspended by Strings Problem Statement Two long straight aluminum wires, each of diameter 0.42 mm, carry the same current but in opposite directions.

item.title: Example 27 - Helmholtz Coils
item.description: Example 27: Helmholtz Coils Problem Statement Two circular coils, each of radius R and carrying the same current I in the same direction, are separated by a distance equal to R (their radius).

item.title: Example 26 - Triangular Loop Near Wire
item.description: Example 26: Force on Triangular Loop Near a Straight Wire Problem Statement A triangular loop of side length a carries a current I.

item.title: Example 25 - Force on Rectangular Loop Near Wire
item.description: Example 25: Force on Rectangular Loop Near a Straight Wire Problem Statement A rectangular loop of wire is placed next to a straight wire.
